name: Lundi Gras Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 27 ounces extra-bittersweet or bittersweet chocolate, chopped
recipeInstructions:
There are two simple tricks to preparing this fancy dessert. First, be certain to use almond paste, not marzipan. Second when making the filling, gently fold the unwhipped cream into the melted chocolate; do not beat.
For cake: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 10x15x-inch jelly roll pan with parchment; butter the parchment. Beat yolks, 3/4 cup sugar, vanilla, and salt with an electric mixer until pale yellow and slowly forming a soft ribbon when beaters are lifted. Beat whites with cream of tartar in another large bowl until soft peaks form. Fold one-third of the whites into yolks to lighten; spoon remaining whites atop yolks. Sift cocoa powder and fold until just combined. Spread in prepared pan. Bake until springy to touch, about 25 minutes.
Meanwhile, if almond paste is dry or crumbly, combine with 1 tablespoon amaretto in a processor and blend until soft enough to roll out, adding more amaretto by teaspoons if necessary. Roll almond paste between 2 sheets of waxed paper into a 9x14-inch rectangle. Refrigerate.
Dust kitchen towel with powdered sugar. Turn cake out onto towel; remove parchment. Trim off edges of cake, leaving about half an inch. Place almond paste on hot cake. Roll the cake up jelly roll fashion, starting at one long side. Cool completely.
Pour remaining 3/4 cup amaretto into a pie plate. Cut cake into 1/3-inch-thick slices. Dip one side of each slice briefly into amaretto. Cover bottom of 10-inch springform pan with some cake slices dipped side down, working from edge to center and arranging open ends of slices facing in the same direction. Press firmly to secure slices. Place remaining slices upright against sides of pan; press firmly to secure. Refrigerate while preparing filling.
For filling: Melt chocolate and butter in a double boiler over barely simmering water, stirring until smooth. Transfer to a large bowl. Immediately add 1 quart cream in three additions, gently folding just until combined; do not beat. Pour into cake-lined pan. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Cut edges of cake even with filling if necessary. Invert cake onto serving platter. Let stand at room temperature for 20 minutes. Spoon whipped cream into a pastry bag fitted with a star tip. Pipe some cream decoratively around the cake. Pass remaining cream separately.
